title: Refactor dev-tools.sh to unified tool-agnostic TOON framework
objective: Replace hardcoded tool functions with single generic framework - all tools defined via config, same external behavior
spirit_anti: Do NOT change external behavior. ./dev-tools.sh ruff must output same TOON format as before. Do NOT add new quality rules or change what tools check.
done_when: All tools (ruff, mypy, pytest, eslint, tsc) use single run_tool_toon() function
TOOL_DEFS array contains config for all 5 tools
Adding a new tool = adding 1 line to TOOL_DEFS
External behavior unchanged: same TOON output format for existing tools

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ruff outputs same TOON format via generic framework: LINT:OK:0 or LINT:FAIL:N|details:...
mypy outputs same TOON format via generic framework: TYPES:OK:0 or TYPES:FAIL:N|details:...
pytest outputs same TOON format via generic framework: TEST:OK:summary or TEST:FAIL:summary|details:...
eslint outputs TOON format via generic framework: ESLINT:OK:0 or ESLINT:FAIL:N|details:...
tsc outputs TOON format via generic framework: TSC:OK:0 or TSC:FAIL:N|details:...

acceptance_criteria.expected_output: 5 (one for each tool: ruff, mypy, pytest, eslint, tsc)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: quick_check includes frontend tools for fullstack projects

decisions.title: Unified framework
decisions.outcome: Replace all hardcoded run_X_toon functions with single generic run_tool_toon()
decisions.rationale: One codebase to maintain. Adding new tool = 1 config line.

decisions.title: Tool definition format
decisions.rationale: Simple bash parsing, no external dependencies.

decisions.title: Preserve external behavior
decisions.outcome: Same TOON output format, same subcommands, same flags
decisions.rationale: No breaking changes to callers (commit.sh, sf-commit.sh, etc.).

constraints: External behavior MUST remain identical: same TOON output format
Subcommands (ruff, mypy, pytest) must continue working
.dev-tools/*.txt detail files must still be created on failure

subtasks.description: Create TOOL_DEFS array and generic run_tool_toon() function
steps: Study existing run_ruff_toon, run_mypy_toon, run_pytest_toon to capture exact behavior
Create run_tool_toon() that: parses config, resolves working dir, runs command, counts errors, writes details file, outputs TOON
Add get_working_dir() helper: backend→backend/ or root, frontend→frontend/ or root
Handle edge cases: tool not found, empty output, non-zero exit without errors

subtasks.description: Migrate ruff, mypy, pytest to TOOL_DEFS
steps: Create parse_pytest_count() helper for pytest's unique output format
Remove old run_ruff_toon(), run_mypy_toon(), run_pytest_toon() functions
Update subcommand case statement to use run_tool_toon
Verify: ./dev-tools.sh ruff, mypy, pytest output identical TOON format

subtasks.description: Add eslint and tsc to TOOL_DEFS
steps: Add eslint and tsc cases to subcommand switch
Add has_frontend() detection: checks for eslint.config.* or tsconfig.json

subtasks.description: Update quick_check and full_check for frontend
steps: Modify quick_check(): after Python checks, if has_frontend(), run eslint and tsc
Modify full_check(): same pattern - add frontend tools after Python
Ensure error counting still works (sum of all tool errors)
Verify: ./dev-tools.sh --quick shows LINT, TYPES, ESLINT, TSC
Add local eslint hook for frontend/
Add local tsc hook for frontend/
Add local prettier hook for frontend/
Test summitflow: ./dev-tools.sh ruff, mypy, pytest, eslint, tsc (all via generic framework)
Test monkey-fight: ./dev-tools.sh tsc (frontend only, no Python)
Verify TOON output format consistent across all

patterns: Working dir types: backend (backend/ or root), frontend (frontend/ or root)
gotchas: pytest has unique output format - needs custom parser for summary line
eslint --format compact gives parseable single-line-per-error output
monkey-fight has no eslint config - only tsc
Must strip ANSI codes from tool output before parsing
